language.description: Preferred language according to ISO 639 two-letter codes

rating.description: Player rating: Each player has a rating that measures their skill level. The rating is continuously calibrated based on the games they play. When two sides play each other, if the winning side's rating average across its players is lower than or equal to that of the opposing side, all players on the winning side receive a rating increase of +1, whereas all players on the losing side receive a rating decrease of -1. If a stronger side beats a weaker side, no rating changes occur.

geolocation.description: GeoJSON point for player's last known location: Whenever a player's device detects its geolocation to be more than 200m away from their current default. It's important to note that this only happens with the app in the foreground, there is no background geolocation tracking. In reality, there isn't even continuous foreground tracking, whenever a feature that invokes geolocation detection naturally is called upon, the client application checks the detected geolocation against the default value in local storage and acts accordingly.

team_id.description: Associated team ID: A player can only be associated to one team at a time. Teams are designed to be ephemeral entities, whenever a team has been inactive (i.e. hasn't recorded any scores) for 4 hours, the team is deleted. The next time a player tries to look the team up by the team ID stored in their database record, this field is cleared. When a player tries to add another player to their team, the application makes sure that the adding player is on a active team and that the added player is not before satisfying the request.

address.description: Address fields for place: The place's address keys and values are listed in order from broadest to narrowest (e.g. { country: ES, region: Andalucía, province: Granada, city: Granada })

parent_ids.description: IDs of parent places, if any: All parent places' IDs are listed in order from broadest to narrowes (e.g. [ID of Spain (Country), ID of Andalucía (Region), ID of Granada (Province)])

name.description: Commonly used court name: Can differ from the court's official name, this should be the colloquial name under which the court is known among local players.

place_ids.description: IDs of associated places: Each court is assigned to the nearest lowest-level place and subsequently assigned to the place's ID as well as all of its parent places

start.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds of the game start time: A game only has a start time and no end time because games always encompass a fixed duration of 1h starting at a full hour. For example, a timestamp that points to 6pm on a certain date means that this game refers to the hour between 6pm and 7pm.

player_ids.description: IDs of signed up players: Games are not treated as events led by someone, but rather as a pool of players who all stated 'I am going to play at X place at Y time'.

court_id.description: ID of associated court: There cannot be two games with the same timestamp and court ID. If a player tries to create a game on a court at a time when a game already exists, they won't be shown an error but instead just added to the existing game.
ID of associated court: Whenever a team is created, its court ID is set to the nearest court, which should be within 200m. This is the reference used to locate nearby teams.

timestamp.description: Unix Timestamp in Milliseconds of the Score Submission Time

points.description: League points for each side: Every time a score is submitted, the winning team earns points based on the rating average of each team. By default, a win grants 5 points, with bonus or penalty points awarded based on the difference in rating averages. For example: If a 6-team and a 4-team play each other, the 6-team stands to win 3 points (5 default - 2 penalty due to weaker opponent) whereas the 4-team stands to win 7 points (5 default + 2 bonus due to stronger opponent). Points assigned are always rounded to the nearest integer. A win grants a minimum of 1 and a maximum of 9 points.

colors.description: Hex Color Codes of Each Side

prefixItems.description: Hex Color Code of Side A (e.g. #20DFBF)
Hex Color Code of Side B (e.g. #DF4020)

confirmations.description: Player IDs of Players who confirmed the Score

rejections.description: Player IDs of Players who rejected the Score

is_confirmed.description: Status of Two-Side Score Confirmation: When a score is submitted, the winning teams' points only count towards league points if both sides have confirmed the score. A score counts as confirmed by a side when more than 50% of the team has confirmed the score (e.g. 3 out of 5 players). In 4vs4 games for example, at least 3 players on each team need to confirm. A score counts as rejected when more then 50% of any of the two teams rejects the score, in which case the game is deleted.

player_ids.description: Player IDs of Players (Flat Version of 'players' field)
